title: Shell integration: generated wrapper code and rc-file edits
summary: `wt config shell init` emits a shell function that the user `eval`s into an interactive shell, and `wt config shell install` edits rc files in place. The emitted wrapper embeds `argv[0]`-derived data (`binary_name`) and the configured command name; anything unescaped that reaches the eval'd output is code injection into the user's shell. rc-file edits must be idempotent and must not corrupt or duplicate an existing managed block. Trust boundary: process-exec (code the shell will run) and filesystem (user rc files).

ownedFiles.reason: shell-integration prompts and install/uninstall result rendering
binary_name/invocation_path: argv[0]-derived values embedded in the emitted wrapper
